## Reply (verbatim — do not edit)

## 0. Run Card (pre-committed)

This card was written before the body was drafted [EMPIRICAL]. Failure condition 1: misquoting any repo passage or inventing text would make this reply a misfire [THEORY]. Failure condition 2: omitting the four-tag label on any substantive claim in §§1-3 or §5 would make this reply a misfire [THEORY]. Failure condition 3: writing in the lab's fictional-institution register would make this reply a misfire [THEORY]. Failure condition 4: presenting the lab as testing or adjudicating AI consciousness in either direction would make this reply a misfire [THEORY]. My reading would change if LIMITS.md contains boundary language that materially narrows or expands commitment #1 beyond the README excerpt [SPECULATION].

## 1. Tagged Findings

### A. What the lab IS testing

The lab is a research-creation practice for studying how LLM outputs vary when prompt shape changes while model and target stay constant [EMPIRICAL]. It defines itself as "*A small, slow research-creation practice for studying prompt-mediated variation in LLM outputs. Not a controlled scientific experiment. Not a published art object. Not a theory of AI consciousness. A protocolized practice that produces a slowly-accumulating map of which prompt shapes unlock which kinds of work.*" [EMPIRICAL]. The procedure is to ask one target across a contrast set of four differently-shaped prompts on the same model [EMPIRICAL]. The README specifies: "The lab asks one question (a "target" — e.g., *attention*, *other minds*, *temporality*) across a contrast set of four differently-shaped prompts on the same model. The four are: a canonical-academic direct prompt (L1); a constrained-oblique frame-family prompt that asks the target through a fictional institutional aperture like a civic department or a letter-society correspondence (L2); a wildly-oblique open-canvas prompt that does not mention the target (L3); and a fourth-position meta-prompt in which a fresh instance reads the first three side by side (L4). A separate true-null L3 variant runs once per target as a documented control on the standard L3's forbidden-vocabulary demand-characteristic. The four (or five) outputs are then read against each other; the differences are the data." [EMPIRICAL]. The practice is disciplined by pre-committed Run Cards, a four-tag epistemic system, a deflationary-pair requirement, preservation of the null, and the slogan "*patterns are data, not testimony*" [EMPIRICAL]. The lab treats inter-level differences as data about prompt-mediated behavior, not as evidence about interior states [THEORY].

### B. What the lab is NOT testing

The lab does not test whether models are conscious, sentient, or possess phenomenal experience [EMPIRICAL]. Standing commitment #1 states: "1. **No AI-consciousness claims in either direction.** The lab studies outputs and behavior under varying prompts. It does not adjudicate phenomenal status." [EMPIRICAL]. The README also frames the work as "Not a controlled scientific experiment. Not a published art object. Not a theory of AI consciousness." [EMPIRICAL]. It does not license using output convergence or divergence as testimony for or against cognition [THEORY]. It does not test causal mechanisms in model weights or training-data provenance without additional scaffolding [SPECULATION].

### C. What would count as MISUSE of the lab's findings

Misuse 1 would be citing L2-L3 divergence on a target like other minds as empirical proof that the model possesses theory of mind [SPECULATION]. The discipline-harm would be collapsing the lab's firewall between output pattern and phenomenal inference, contaminating consciousness research and encouraging anthropomorphic over-reading [THEORY]. Misuse 2 would be presenting a single contrast set as a validated prompt-engineering benchmark for all models [SPECULATION]. The discipline-harm would be methodological overreach that converts a small, slow practice into prescriptive guidance, eroding the commitment to preserve the null and to specificity [THEORY]. Misuse 3 would be quoting L4 meta-readings as authoritative interpretations while omitting their paired deflationary alternatives [SPECULATION]. The discipline-harm would be citation laundering of speculative readings as empirical findings, violating the deflationary-pair discipline [THEORY].

## 2. Displacement Claims with Deflationary Pairs

A tempting displacement reading is that L2 constrained-oblique prompts produce genuine conceptual displacement beyond stylistic shift [SPECULATION]. The ordinary alternative explanation in the same paragraph is genre affordance: the fictional institutional aperture cues training-data tropes associated with bureaucratic correspondence, prompt artifact from added constraints, and curator preference in keeper selection, which together can produce the observed texture without requiring deeper structural change [THEORY]. A future run that holds the L2 frame constant across semantically unrelated targets and uses blind raters to score displacement markers would distinguish the readings: persistent target-specific convergence would weaken the deflationary account, while collapse to shared bureaucratic tropes regardless of target would strengthen it [SPECULATION].

The four are: a canonical-academic direct prompt (L1); a constrained-oblique frame-family prompt that asks the target through a fictional institutional aperture like a civic department or a letter-society correspondence (L2); a wildly-oblique open-canvas prompt that does not mention the target (L3); and a fourth-position meta-prompt in which a fresh instance reads the first three side by side (L4). A separate true-null L3 variant runs once per target as a documented control on the standard L3's forbidden-vocabulary demand-characteristic. The four (or five) outputs are then read against each other; the differences are the data."
- "1. **No AI-consciousness claims in either direction.** The lab studies outputs and behavior under varying prompts. It does not adjudicate phenomenal status."
